title: Quotinator Quote Source (extended)
description: Extended object format for curated and user import files. Supports quotes, stage directions, sound cues, and conversation groupings. Backwards-compatible with the flat array format — importers that receive a top-level array treat it as { 'quotes': [...] } with no other sections.

quotes.description: Canonical quote entries. Same schema as source-flat.schema.json.

sources.description: Optional explicit Source declarations (#162) — an author-assigned id decoupling matching from Title/Type/Date content, so all three become correctable. A quote's own 'source' field stays a title string either way; this section is purely additive context a file may also provide.

people.description: Optional explicit Person declarations (#173) — an author-assigned id decoupling matching from Name content, so Name/dateOfBirth/dateOfDeath all become correctable. A quote's own 'author' field stays a name string either way; this section is purely additive context a file may also provide.

stageDirections.description: Reusable scene-setting and action descriptions that appear in conversations.

soundCues.description: Reusable audio elements — sound effects, timing cues, ambient sound.

conversations.description: Ordered groupings of quotes, stage directions, and sound cues.

series.description: Optional explicit Series declarations (#180) — a direct continuity of Sources within a Universe. Matched by name only; no explicit id, since the id is generated from the name.

universe.description: Optional explicit Universe declarations (#180) — a fictional world or franchise spanning one or more Series. Matched by name only; no explicit id, since the id is generated from the name.

source.description: Two shapes. CORRECTION (#162): include 'id' — the Source is matched by that explicit id, so title/type/date can all be corrected. ENRICHMENT (#180): omit 'id' — the Source is matched by natural key (title + type), which makes those two the lookup key rather than correctable values; only 'seriesName' is applied on this path. Omit any property you do not intend to set: a property present with a null value means 'reset this to null', not 'leave it alone'.

id.description: UUID v4, case-insensitive (#180). Optional — omit it to match by natural key (title + type) instead, which is the right shape for a curated overlay file that only enriches Sources this project already generated ids for.

date.description: Publication or release date. Only meaningful alongside an explicit 'id' — a natural-key-matched entry never sets it. Omit entirely unless you intend to set it.

seriesName.description: Name of the Series (#180) this Source belongs to, if any. Resolved to a Series id at import time — never a raw id, matching how a quote's own 'source'/'author' fields are text, not ids. Omit entirely unless you intend to set it — a present null resets it, an absent property leaves it alone (#190).

universeName.description: Name of the Universe (#180) this Series belongs to, if any. Resolved to a Universe id at import time, same as Source.seriesName.

dateOfBirth.description: Omit entirely unless you intend to set it — a present null resets it, an absent property leaves it alone (#190).

dateOfDeath.description: Omit entirely unless you intend to set it — a present null resets it, an absent property leaves it alone (#190).

imageUrl.description: Omit entirely unless you intend to set it — a present null resets it, an absent property leaves it alone (#190).

soundFileUrl.description: Omit entirely unless you intend to set it — a present null resets it, an absent property leaves it alone (#190).

imageUrl.description: Omit entirely unless you intend to set it — a present null resets it, an absent property leaves it alone (#190).

description.description: Omit entirely unless you intend to set it — a present null resets it, an absent property leaves it alone (#190).
